title: Machaeranthera wigginsii
intro: Machaeranthera wigginsii, also known as Wiggins' tansy aster, is a flowering plant species belonging to the daisy family, Asteraceae. The plant is valued for its potential health benefits, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which may help alleviate symptoms of various conditions such as arthritis and skin infections. The bioactive constituents of Machaeranthera wigginsii include sesquiterpene lactones, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. Traditionally, the plant is used to make herbal teas, tinctures, and infusions, which are commonly used to treat colds, fever, and respiratory issues.
taxonomy: Machaeranthera wigginsii is classified as a species of flowering plant in the family Asteraceae. This plant is native to the southwestern United States and northern Mexico. Its classification is further divided into the tribe Astereae and the genus Machaeranthera. The species name wigginsii honors the American botanist Robert P. Wiggins. Machaeranthera wigginsii is considered an endemic species, meaning it is found only in a specific geographic region.
